description_long: This house features a modern, minimalist design with a sharply angled, dark-colored gable roof and a striking front facade dominated by a large grid of floor-to-ceiling glass panels. The front entryway is defined by a sleek, single door with a curved transom window, set within the glass wall. The side and rear elevations are mostly solid, finished in a smooth, light-colored material, with minimal window openings and a single rectangular protrusion on one side. The interior, visible through the glass, appears to be open-plan with contemporary furnishings. The overall structure is compact and geometric, emphasizing clean lines and transparency. The house is likely constructed with a combination of glass, metal, and concrete or composite panels.
description: A modern house with a large glass front facade, dark gable roof, and minimalist solid side walls. The entryway features a sleek door set within the glass grid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the expansive glass facade with grid pattern and the main entry door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a solid, light-colored wall with no windows and a clean, uninterrupted surface.
description_view_2: Rear or opposite side view with a solid wall and a rectangular protrusion, minimal window presence.
description_view_3: Back view with mostly solid dark wall, minimal architectural features visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: large picture windows in a grid pattern on the front facade
attribute_entryway_design: Single modern door with curved transom, integrated into glass facade, no porch or steps visible
